meaning: n.斯基台人[语]adj.斯基台的,斯基台人[语]的
examples: The Scythian Pazyryk of the Altai Mountain region were another ancient culture which employed tattoos.
Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond [nor] free: but Christ [is] all, and in all.
In 1948, the 2, 400 year old body of a Scythian male was discovered preserved in ice in Siberia, his limbs and torso covered in ornate tattoos of mythical animals.
Scythian "flying stag" discovered in a Cossack village in 1897 has become a logo piece for the museum.
Virtually all of the ancient gold in the Galleries, Scythian or otherwise, was unearthed from burial mounds scattered across Russia from Siberia to the Ukraine.
